<!-- .github/copilot-instructions.md -->
# Repo orientation for AI coding agents

This is a small, static, bilingual portfolio built with Astro (framework + Vite). Keep instructions short, actionable and project-specific — below are the patterns and files that matter.

- **Big picture**: Astro static site with reusable components and Markdown content collections. Layouts live in `src/layouts/`, pages in `src/pages/` (Spanish mirrors under `src/pages/es/`), reusable UI in `src/components/` and content in `src/content/` (see `projects` and `experience`, each split into `en/` and `es/` subfolders). Netlify is used for deployment (`netlify.toml`).

- **How to run**:
  - `npm install` (the project runs `npm install` as `prebuild`)
  - `npm run dev` — start Astro dev server (hot reload)
  - `npm run build` — produce production output in `dist` (Netlify publishes `dist`)
  - `npm run preview` — preview the built output
  - Node 22 is used in CI/deploy (`netlify.toml`)

- **i18n**:
  - English is the default locale at `/`; Spanish lives at `/es/` (configured in `astro.config.mjs`).
  - All UI strings come from the dictionary in `src/i18n/ui.ts`; helpers (`getLangFromUrl`, `useTranslations`, `localizePath`, `getAlternatePath`) are in `src/i18n/utils.ts`.
  - Components self-localize from the URL; the header's `LanguageToggle.astro` swaps the `/es` prefix, so page slugs must match across locales.
  - The resume PDF is locale-specific (`/HvTmpEnglish.pdf` / `/HvTmpSpanish.pdf`, via the `hero.cvFile` dictionary key).

- **Content & pages**:
  - Content collections: `src/content/projects/{en,es}` and `src/content/experience/{en,es}` are read with `getCollection()` filtered by slug prefix (see [src/pages/projects.astro](src/pages/projects.astro)).
  - Markdown frontmatter fields used by the site: `title`, `description`, `date`, `img`, `url`, `author`, `technologies` (array), `tags`, `order`.
  - Projects listing sorts by `order` (default fallback `999`); keep `order` in sync between the `en` and `es` versions of a project.
  - Individual project pages are generated by the dynamic routes `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro` and `src/pages/es/projects/[slug].astro`, rendered through [src/layouts/MarkdownPostLayout.astro](src/layouts/MarkdownPostLayout.astro). To add a project, create the markdown file in **both** `src/content/projects/en/` and `src/content/projects/es/` with the same filename.

- **Components & patterns**:
  - Components are `.astro` files in `src/components/`. React components are `.tsx` under `src/components/ui/` and require the `@astrojs/react` integration (configured in `astro.config.mjs`).
  - Use the TS path alias `@/*` (maps to `src/*`) from `tsconfig.json`. Example: `import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"`.
  - SVGs are imported directly from `src/assets/svg/` (Vite handles them). Never import SVGs from `/public` — files there are for URL references only.

- **Styling & theme**:
  - Tailwind + handcrafted CSS variables are used. Global styles: [src/styles/global.css](src/styles/global.css) and [src/styles/tokens.css](src/styles/tokens.css).
  - Theme toggling uses a `data-theme` attribute and `localStorage`. See the inline script in [src/layouts/Layout.astro](src/layouts/Layout.astro) and [src/components/Switch.astro](src/components/Switch.astro).

- **Build & deploy details**:
  - Netlify configuration is [netlify.toml](netlify.toml): `npm run build`, publish `dist`, `NODE_VERSION=22`.
  - SEO: `site` is set in `astro.config.mjs`; `@astrojs/sitemap` generates the sitemap, `public/robots.txt` points to it, and `Layout.astro` emits canonical + hreflang alternates.

- **What is NOT present**:
  - There are no automated tests or lint scripts in the repository — add CI tooling consciously and document it.

- **When editing**:
  - Add content markdown to `src/content/*` (projects or experience) in **both** locale folders if you want the item to show up in collections.
  - Prefer using the existing layout components (`Layout.astro`, `MarkdownPostLayout.astro`) and style tokens from `tokens.css`.
  - Never hardcode user-facing text in components — add a key to `src/i18n/ui.ts` (both `en` and `es`) and use `t()`.
